Why Choose an Insurance Roofer?

Your Insurance Company Has Experts.
You Should Too.

Most homeowners don't realize their roof has sustained wind or hail damage until it starts leaking. By then, it might be too late to file a claim. Standard roofing contractors will simply give you a retail quote.

As insurance restoration specialists, our goal is different: We find the damage, document it, and work directly with your insurance adjuster to ensure you get the full replacement you are entitled to under your policy.

  • We meet the adjuster on-site To point out damage they might accidentally "miss".
  • We speak their language We use Xactimate, the same estimating software insurance uses.
  • No surprises We do the work for the exact amount your insurance agrees to pay.

Our Process

From Damage to Done in 4 Steps

We make replacing your roof through insurance completely stress-free. Here is how we turn a storm disaster into a brand new roof.

1

Free Inspection

We perform a comprehensive drone and physical inspection to document all storm damage.

2

Filing the Claim

If damage is found, we assist you in calling your insurance agent to properly file the claim.

3

Adjuster Meeting

We meet the insurance adjuster on your roof to advocate for you and ensure all damage is covered.

4

Build Day

Once approved, we install your beautiful new roof in typically 1-2 days, leaving your yard spotless.

Signs You Have an Insurance Claim

You don't need a massive hole in your roof to qualify. Insurance companies look for specific types of damage that compromise the integrity of the shingle.

Hail Damage

Hail knocks the protective granules off shingles, exposing the asphalt to UV rays which causes leaks over time. It's often invisible from the ground.

Wind Damage

High winds can lift shingles, breaking the sealant seal. Once the seal is broken, water can blow under the shingles during the next storm.

Missing Shingles

The most obvious sign of damage. If you see shingles in your yard or bald spots on your roof, you have a highly viable insurance claim.

Knowledge is Power

Insurance Claim FAQs

Will filing a claim raise my insurance rates?

Storm damage is considered an "Act of God" by insurance companies. Typically, they cannot single you out for a rate increase because you filed a weather-related claim. Rates usually go up by zip code based on the storm hitting the area, regardless of whether you file or not.

How much will this cost me?

If your claim is approved, your only out-of-pocket expense is your deductible, which was agreed upon when you purchased your policy. We do the work for the exact amount your insurance company agrees to pay.

What if my claim was already denied?

Call us! Insurance adjusters make mistakes or overlook damage. We offer free second opinions and can request a re-inspection with a different adjuster, armed with our photo report and evidence.
